feedback.text: Hello, Slightly longer than a week ago Google announced that starting next year android app developers will have to be verified by Google in order to install applications in Android devices running Google Play Services (pretty much all the consumer Android smartphones and tablets sold in Europe). Right now when installing an app that Google considers unsafe there is already a warning notifying the risk but you are ultimately still allowed to install the app, in the future this won't be the case. This will impact ALL applications, even outside the Play Store, so alternative stores (stores that aren't Google's) and sideloading (directly installing the app on your smartphone) are affected and developers will be required to pay a 25$ fee too Google to be verified (giving all their personal information to Google) while currently developers are only required to pay to release their app on the Google Play Store. If for whatever reason a developer cannot be verified his application pretty much won't install. As an Android developer myself and having seen Google behavior towards other colleagues in the past I don't trust Google or any non governative entity with any kind of power in deciding whose app I can or cannot install on my device and this strictly limits some of the uses the hardware I previous bought.
